title: Customer log intake normalization v2
description: Improve how NEXUS handles noisy real customer incidents by deepening log normalization, partial-input handling, and tenant-aware intake hints. The goal is to make fresh tenant incidents more usable before triage quality and proof expectations are evaluated.
implementation_tasks: improve parsing and normalization for messier tenant-provided logs
add bounded tenant-aware input hints or profiles where useful
improve fallback behavior for partial, low-signal, or malformed inputs
make intake quality and normalization posture clearer to operators
done_when: fresh customer incidents are normalized more usefully before triage
partial or weak input quality is surfaced clearly instead of failing silently
the product handles noisier pilot inputs more credibly

title: Coverage matrix and unsupported-incident downgrade path
description: Make support coverage explicit per tenant and per incident family. The product should clearly distinguish runtime-backed support, inference-first support, and unsupported cases, and it should downgrade gracefully instead of bluffing when a case falls outside the current wedge.
implementation_tasks: define per-tenant support-state representation for runtime-backed, inference-first, and unsupported cases
add downgrade semantics and operator guidance for unsupported incidents
surface coverage matrix information in settings, training, or incident surfaces
keep the product truthfulness model visible in downgrade flows
done_when: operators can see what the tenant is strongly supported for
unsupported incidents degrade honestly and clearly
the product does not overclaim when runtime support is unavailable

title: Fresh-incident quality evaluation harness
description: Add a bounded evaluation harness so the team can assess how good NEXUS is on fresh incidents during pilots. The product should make it possible to review issue framing, owner routing, next-step quality, and uncertainty language instead of only trusting informal impressions.
implementation_tasks: add a bounded fresh-incident review and scoring structure
capture issue framing, owner routing, next-step quality, and uncertainty quality
support lightweight reviewer feedback for fresh-incident evaluation
keep evaluation output usable for pilot learning without becoming a full analytics platform
done_when: fresh-incident quality can be evaluated in a structured way
the team can review triage quality beyond seeded incidents
the harness helps pilot learning without broadening scope excessively

title: Pilot scorecard dashboard
description: Make pilot value visible at the tenant level with a durable scorecard. Buyers and operators should be able to see incidents handled, runtime-backed versus inferred-only ratios, triage time saved, handoff completion, and repeat-incident reuse without relying only on presentation narration.
implementation_tasks: expose per-tenant pilot scorecard signals
add a scorecard section or surface that is legible to operators and buyers
keep scorecard metrics tied to measured or honestly estimated evidence
prepare the scorecard for use in weekly pilot reviews
done_when: pilot value is visible in a durable per-tenant scorecard
the scorecard is useful to both operators and buyers
value proof is less dependent on static docs alone

title: Case-based proof export
description: Generate proof artifacts from real handled incidents rather than relying only on curated examples. The product should export case-based before/after summaries, handoff quality proof, runtime validation proof, and value summaries that can be used in pilot reviews and buyer conversations.
implementation_tasks: add exportable proof packaging based on real handled incidents
support before/after case framing and evidence-backed value summaries
make proof exports buyer-usable while preserving truthful evidence posture
tie proof exports back to incident history and scorecard signals where useful
done_when: real pilot cases can be exported as proof artifacts
buyers can review evidence-backed case summaries instead of only hearing narration
the proof export remains bounded and honest about support state

title: Engineering handoff trust v3
description: Deepen engineering trust by improving repo-aware inspection guidance, mitigation rationale, and residual-risk framing. The goal is to make the handoff feel more concrete for engineering teams while still staying bounded to the supported wedge rather than pretending to be a universal debugger.
implementation_tasks: strengthen repo-aware inspection briefs and likely inspection targets
improve mitigation rationale and residual-risk framing
make the engineering handoff packet more concrete about what to inspect next and why
keep the debugger story explicitly bounded
done_when: engineering handoffs feel more actionable and trustworthy
inspect-first guidance is easier to follow
the handoff becomes stronger without overclaiming debugging breadth

title: Runtime evidence weighting v3
description: Deepen how inference, memory, and runtime evidence are weighted so the product’s recommendations feel more defensible in real pilots. The product should show more clearly when an action is inferred, improved by runtime validation, or strongly runtime-resolved, with tighter confidence and residual-risk semantics.
implementation_tasks: deepen weighting between inference, memory, and runtime evidence
improve recommendation posture on resolved, improved, and unvalidated cases
tighten residual-risk and confidence semantics
make recommendation quality more visible in product surfaces and exported packets
done_when: runtime evidence more clearly drives recommendations
operators and reviewers can understand why an action won
the product feels more defensible in runtime-backed cases

title: Pilot operations kit and FR2 checkpoint
description: Close FR2 with a deliberate multi-tenant pilot checkpoint. Add the pilot operations kit, run the full validation stack, and refresh the owner-facing control docs so the repo ends the phase with a clear repeatable-enterprise-pilot posture before any selective outage expansion begins.
implementation_tasks: add the pilot operations kit including tenant setup runbook, operator quickstart, weekly pilot review checklist, and pilot closeout template
run the full validation stack across tests, browser flows, demo flow, and packaged-app Docker path
refresh owner-facing docs so the FR2 posture is current
refresh control docs so backlog-101-plus.json becomes the closed phase baseline
done_when: the repo ends the FR2 phase with a clean validated checkpoint
the pilot operations kit exists and is usable
control docs are current and ready for the next planning phase
